summary: Iteration 15 — final site-wide polish (footer, legal pages, launch-checklist, 404, robots/sitemap, homepage SEO). FULL VERIFICATION PASSED. Backend pytest 21/21 green (2.47s). All 17 frontend assertions from the review request passed: robots.txt (200, has 'User-agent: *', 'Disallow: /launch-checklist', 'Sitemap: https://kobiisrael.life/sitemap.xml'); sitemap.xml (200, exactly 14 <url> entries, valid XML); homepage title + meta description match spec; 4-col footer present on every page; 10 footer-explore-*, 5 footer-inquiry-* (all → /contact#contact-form-section), 4 footer-follow-* (all 'To be confirmed by artist'), 5 footer-legal-* (all → /legal/<slug>); copyright bottom bar contains all 3 required strings; all 5 legal pages (privacy/terms/copyright/cookies/accessibility) render with correct titles, h1, ≥3 legal-section-* per page, and the 'Placeholder text · final wording to be reviewed before launch' line (verified via textContent — CSS text-transform: uppercase is presentation only); /legal/unknown-slug correctly falls through to NotFound; /launch-checklist has title='Launch Checklist | Kobi Israel', meta robots='noindex, nofollow', 5 launch-section-*, 43 launch-item-*, intro mentions 'noindex'; /this-does-not-exist renders both 'not-found-page' and 'project-not-found' testids with H1='Page Not Found' and 3 correctly-targeted CTAs; /projects/<bad-slug> preserves the iteration_12 'project-not-found' invariant; footer→/legal/privacy click works, legal-related-terms cross-link works, footer-inquiry-collector navigates to /contact#contact-form-section. All 12 regression routes load with footer and 0 console errors. Mobile 390x844 has no horizontal overflow on /, /launch-checklist, /legal/privacy.
critical_code_review_comments: Footer.jsx, NotFound.jsx, Legal.jsx, LaunchChecklist.jsx are all clean, small (<210 lines), and follow project conventions (data-testid coverage, env-driven URLs, Tailwind classes consistent with the rest of the site).
NotFound.jsx renders BOTH a primary section[data-testid='not-found-page'] AND an sr-only div[data-testid='project-not-found'] — iteration_12 backward-compat invariant is preserved correctly.

Minor non-blocking observation: the section element also carries an invalid HTML attribute `data-testid-alias='project-not-found'` (custom attribute is fine but unused — the sr-only sibling div is the actual selector source). Safe to leave or remove.

robots.txt at the public preview URL is served via Cloudflare and prepends Cloudflare-managed Content-Signal directives BEFORE the project's own block. The project's required directives ('User-agent: *', 'Disallow: /launch-checklist', 'Sitemap:' line) are all present, so spec requirements are met — but be aware that on the preview origin the file is augmented by the infra layer. The static file in /app/frontend/public/robots.txt is exactly what was authored.
Sitemap and robots reference https://kobiisrael.life/ (production domain) rather than the preview URL — this is correct for production SEO and was the intended behaviour.
Legal.jsx and LaunchChecklist.jsx have a useEffect cleanup that restores prev document.title — this is correct and avoids leaking the title to the next route mount.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (every assertion from the iteration_15 review request verified: 4 footer cols, 10+5+4+5=24 footer testids, 5 legal pages × {title, h1, ≥3 sections, placeholder phrase}, launch-checklist 5/43, 404 with both testids + 3 CTAs, /projects/<bad> backward-compat, 2 navigation flows, 12 regression routes w/ 0 console errors, mobile 3 routes w/ no horizontal overflow)

test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None new this iteration; pytest run creates TEST_ prefixed inquiries as before.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 15: full site-wide polish verified — backend 21/21, frontend 100% on the spec checklist. The iteration_12 'project-not-found' invariant is preserved by an sr-only div inside the new NotFound. /launch-checklist is doubly protected (robots.txt Disallow + per-page meta noindex,nofollow). Note for any future test that asserts text on .overline / launch-checklist intro / legal placeholder line: those elements use Tailwind `uppercase` CSS text-transform, so Playwright `inner_text()` returns ALL CAPS — use page.evaluate("el.textContent") or compare case-insensitively. No outstanding issues.
